extraction.one_line_summary: WOODS Michael won from a late breakaway group by counterattacking with ~10km remaining after gambling on catching the earlier escape.
tactical_patterns: EF used COURT Magnus as decoy in early break while WOODS counterattacked from chasing group, splitting opponents' marking responsibilities.
INEOS committed AMADOR to extended chase work protecting CARAPAZ red jersey, revealing defensive resource allocation for GC stages.
KOM points contested aggressively 12+ miles from finish despite breakaway context, showing jersey competition overriding stage win tactics.

rider_signals.WOODS Michael: positive: attacked from chase group with Valverde/Freila to bridge gap, then won sprint despite sub-4 miler track background suggesting pure power
rider_signals.AMADOR Andre: positive: deployed for extended chase work by INEOS protecting CARAPAZ, indicating elite domestique form and team GC protection strategy
rider_signals.COURT Magnus: positive: willing to sacrifice own stage chance by staying in early break as decoy for teammate WOODS, demonstrating tactical selflessness
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: negative: burned matches trying to defend KOM jersey at intermediate climb, finished out of winning position despite early break presence
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: moved from 3min GC deficit into breakaway and 9th overall, showing opportunistic placement despite being 40 years old
